result.stdout:
[11:50:27 INF] ABP CLI (https://abp.io)
[11:50:27 INF] Version 2.9.0-dev (Development channel)
[11:50:27 INF] 
Usage:

    abp <command> <target> [options]

Command List:

    > help: Show command line help. Write ` abp help <command> `
    > new: Generate a new solution based on the ABP startup templates.
    > get-source: Download the source code of the specified module.
    > update: Update all ABP related NuGet packages and NPM packages in a solution or project to the latest version.
    > add-package: Add a new ABP package to a project by adding related NuGet package dependencies and [DependsOn(...)] attributes.
    > add-module: Add a multi-package module to a solution by finding all packages of the module, finding related projects in the solution and adding each package to the corresponding project in the solution.
    > login: Sign in to https://account.abp.io/.
    > logout: Sign out from https://account.abp.io/.
    > generate-proxy: Generates typescript service proxies and DTOs
    > suite: Install, update, remove or start ABP Suite. See https://commercial.abp.io/tools/suite.
    > switch-to-preview: Switches packages to nightly preview ABP version.
    > switch-to-stable: Switches packages to stable ABP version from preview version.
    > translate: Mainly used to translate ABP's resources (JSON files) easier.
    > rename: rename project namespace

To get a detailed help for a command:

    abp help <command>

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.abp.io/en/abp/latest/CLI

result.stdout:
[11:50:30 INF] ABP CLI (https://abp.io)
[11:50:30 INF] Version 2.9.0-dev (Development channel)
[11:50:30 WRN] Module name is missing!


'add-module' command is used to add a multi-package ABP module to a solution.
It should be used in a folder containing a .sln file.

Usage:
  abp add-module <module-name> [options]

Options:
  --with-source-code                              Downloads the source code of the module and adds it to your solution.
  -s|--solution <solution-file>                   Specify the solution file explicitly.
  --skip-db-migrations <boolean>                  Specify if a new migration will be added or not.
  -sp|--startup-project <startup-project-path>    Relative path to the project folder of the startup project. Default value is the current folder.

Examples:

  abp add-module Volo.Blogging                      Adds the module to the current solution.
  abp add-module Volo.Blogging -s Acme.BookStore    Adds the module to the given solution.
  abp add-module Volo.Blogging -s Acme.BookStore --skip-db-migrations false    Adds the module to the given solution but doesn't create a database migration.
  abp add-module Volo.Blogging -s Acme.BookStore -sp ..\Acme.BookStore.Web\Acme.BookStore.Web.csproj   Adds the module to the given solution and specify migration startup project.

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.abp.io/en/abp/latest/CLI

'add-package' command is used to add an ABP package to a project.
It should be used in a folder containing a .csproj file.

Usage:

  abp add-package <package-name> [options]

Options:

  -p|--project <project-file>    Specify the project file explicitly.

Examples:

  abp add-package Volo.Abp.FluentValidation                                  Adds the package to the current project.
  abp add-package Volo.Abp.FluentValidation -p Acme.BookStore.Application    Adds the package to the given project.

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.abp.io/en/abp/latest/CLI

[11:50:28 INF] ABP CLI (https://abp.io)
[11:50:28 INF] Version 2.9.0-dev (Development channel)
[11:50:28 WRN] Project name is missing!


Usage:

  abp new <project-name> [options]

Options:

-t|--template <template-name>               (default: app)
-u|--ui <ui-framework>                      (if supported by the template)
-m|--mobile <mobile-framework>              (if supported by the template)
-d|--database-provider <database-provider>  (if supported by the template)
-o|--output-folder <output-folder>          (default: current folder)
-v|--version <version>                      (default: latest version)
-ts|--template-source <template-source>     (your local or network abp template source)
-csf|--create-solution-folder               (default: true)
-cs|--connection-string <connection-string> (your database connection string)
--tiered                                    (if supported by the template)
--no-ui                                     (if supported by the template)
--separate-identity-server                  (if supported by the template)
--local-framework-ref --abp-path <your-local-abp-repo-path>  (keeps local references to projects instead of replacing with NuGet package references)

Examples:

  abp new Acme.BookStore
  abp new Acme.BookStore --tiered
  abp new Acme.BookStore -u angular
  abp new Acme.BookStore -u angular -d mongodb
  abp new Acme.BookStore -m none
  abp new Acme.BookStore -m react-native
  abp new Acme.BookStore -d mongodb
  abp new Acme.BookStore -d mongodb -o d:\my-project
  abp new Acme.BookStore -t module
  abp new Acme.BookStore -t module --no-ui
  abp new Acme.BookStore -ts "D:\localTemplate\abp"
  abp new Acme.BookStore -csf false
  abp new Acme.BookStore --local-framework-ref --abp-path "D:\github\abp"
  abp new Acme.BookStore --connection-string "Server=myServerName\myInstanceName;Database=myDatabase;User Id=myUsername;Password=myPassword"

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.abp.io/en/abp/latest/CLI

Usage:

  abp suite [options]

Options:

<no argument>                          (run ABP Suite)
install                                (install ABP Suite as a dotnet global tool)
update                                 (update ABP Suite to the latest)
remove                                 (uninstall ABP Suite)

Examples:

  abp suite
  abp suite install
  abp suite update
  abp suite remove

[11:50:49 INF] ABP CLI (https://abp.io)
[11:50:49 INF] Version 2.9.0-dev (Development channel)
[11:50:49 WRN] Target culture is missing!


Usage:

  abp translate [options]

Options:

--culture|-c <culture>                       Target culture. eg: zh-Hans
--reference-culture|-r <culture>             Default: en
--output|-o <file-name>                      Output file name, Default abp-translation.json
--all-values|-all                            Include all keys. Default false
--apply|-a                                   Creates or updates the file for the translated culture.
--file|-f <file-name>                        Default: abp-translation.json

Examples:

  abp translate -c zh-Hans
  abp translate -c zh-Hans -r en -a
  abp translate --apply
  abp translate -a -f my-translation.json

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.abp.io/en/abp/latest/CLI

Usage:

  abp update [options]

Options:
-p|--include-previews                       (if supported by the template)
--npm                                       (Only updates NPM packages)
--nuget                                     (Only updates Nuget packages)
-sp|--solution-path                         (Specify the solution path)
-sn|--solution-name                         (Specify the solution name)

Some examples:

  abp update
  abp update -p
  abp update -sp "D:\projects\" -sn Acme.BookStore

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.abp.io/en/abp/latest/CLI
